skillName: Position negatives on a number line
misconceptionSummary: Students place negative numbers in ascending digit order to the left of zero, so −1 ends up furthest left instead of −5.

steps.narration: The student placed negative one at the far left and negative five near zero — treating the digits as if negative numbers increase toward the left in the same order as positives. That is back to front.

visuals.ruleText: Count outward from zero — negative numbers grow away from zero to the left
visuals.subText: −1 is the first step left; −5 is five steps left

steps.narration: Always start placing from zero. Negative one is the first step left — it is the closest negative to zero. Negative five is five steps left — it is the furthest from zero of the two.
Negative one sits at the first tick left of zero. Negative five sits at the fifth tick — the far left end. The digit tells you how many steps from zero, always counting left.

visuals.label: Count steps left from zero — bigger digit = further left

steps.narration: Negative one is close to zero; negative five is far from it. The digit is a distance from zero — count that many steps to the left.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: placing −1 furthest left because 1 comes before 5. Start at zero and count LEFT — −1 is just one step away; −5 is five steps away.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Negative numbers grow away from zero to the left. Count from zero — the digit tells you the distance, the minus sign tells you to go left.
